response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and web developer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which the resume does not indicate (languages listed are English, Romanian, and German). Core technical requirements are also unmet: there is no evidence of Linux kernel investigation or modification, no embedded networking or LAN-switch firmware work, and no exposure to the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). C experience exists but is brief and dated (roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014-2015), far from the strong UNIX C proficiency required.
strengths: Broad, sustained software engineering experience (15+ years) across web, mobile, desktop, and some systems-level work
Some C/C++ background (NI LabWindows/CVI components, audio thesis project on Linux) and low-latency systems work in DLang
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and open-source contributions
concerns: No indication of Japanese language ability, a hard requirement for this role
No embedded firmware, Linux kernel, or L2/L3 networking/switch development experience
No experience with the required security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, authentication systems, tamper detection); C skills are minimal and not recent

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking / Linux kernel / security-protocol experience
